headline: 'We are walking a fine line': Incurably ill patients on France's new assisted-dying law
description: A law creating a right to aid in dying was adopted by French lawamkers on Wednesday. Le Monde spoke with people facing serious illness about how the choice weighs on their lives.

headline: French parliament adopts assisted dying law supported by Macron

René Obermann, chairman of Airbus: 'We must put an end to industrial nationalism in defense'
description: In an interview with Le Monde and Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung, the chair of the board of the European aerospace and defense group expressed concern about the future of French-German cooperation, which has been undermined by the cancellation in June of the FCAS, the next-generation fighter jet project.

headline: Christopher Nolan struggles to match Homer in 'The Odyssey'
description: In his search for an original aesthetic capable of capturing the epic masterpiece of antiquity, the filmmaker opts for grandeur, with a prestigious cast and vast resources, but fails to convince.

headline: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup 2026 final
description: Argentina will have a chance to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups after Lionel Messi, 39, once again dragged his team to victory.

headline: 2026 World Cup: How France's American dream built up speed – and crashed
description: France fell hard to Spain on Tuesday in the semifinals of the football tournament, marking a cruel and unexpected ending for Didier Deschamps' squad, who had delighted supporters since the start of the competition.

From Paris to Nice, Macron marks his final Bastille Day with a series of commemorations
description: For his final national holiday as president, Emmanuel Macron presided over an unprecedented military parade and paid solemn tribute to the victims of the Nice terror attack.

headline: Ukraine expands attacks in Sea of Azov, a crucial maritime corridor for Russia
description: After choking off Crimea, which Moscow has occupied since 2014, Ukrainian drones are waging a relentless naval campaign against Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet,' which has been accused of evading sanctions. This has brought the traffic of these vessels on the Sea of Azov to a near halt.

headline: Visualize the full extent of wildfires across Europe in recent weeks
description: The burned areas across the European continent have already far surpassed levels seen in recent years and are now approaching the maximum recorded in 2025.
